proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with stage_iv = 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those with stage_iv = 0.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope in OLS).
Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Female sex (sex_female = 1) is associated with longer pfs_months than male.
Right-sided primary (right_sided_primary = 1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS stage_iv=1: 3.703 vs stage_iv=0: 5.055; diff=-1.352, p=0
Slope of ecog_ps on pfs_months = -1.1897, p=0
Mean PFS female: 4.308 vs male: 4.314; diff=-0.006, p=0.77
Mean PFS right-sided: 4.114 vs left-sided: 4.419; diff=-0.305, p=1.04e-49

proposed_hypotheses.text: kras_mutation = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than kras_mutation = 0.
nras_mutation = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than nras_mutation = 0.
braf_v600e = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than braf_v600e = 0.
msi_high = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than msi_high = 0.
her2_amplified = 1 has different mean pfs_months than her2_amplified = 0.
ntrk_fusion = 1 has different mean pfs_months than ntrk_fusion = 0.
Patients with treatment_cetuximab = 1 have a different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_cetuximab = 0 (overall).
Patients with treatment_bevacizumab = 1 have a different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_bevacizumab = 0 (overall).
Patients with treatment_pembrolizumab = 1 have a different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_pembrolizumab = 0 (overall).
Patients with treatment_encorafenib = 1 have a different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_encorafenib = 0 (overall).
Patients with treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib = 1 have a different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib = 0 (overall).
Patients with treatment_regorafenib = 1 have a different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_regorafenib = 0 (overall).

calcium_mg_dl is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
There is a positive treatment_cetuximab × kras_mutation=0 interaction on pfs_months: cetuximab works only in KRAS wild-type patients.
There is a positive treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high interaction on pfs_months: pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in MSI-high patients.
There is a positive treatment_encorafenib × braf_v600e interaction on pfs_months: encorafenib benefit is concentrated in BRAF V600E patients.
There is a positive treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib × her2_amplified interaction on pfs_months: benefit is concentrated in HER2-amplified patients.
Cetuximab benefit further depends on right_sided_primary status: it is greater in left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0) within KRAS wild-type patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_bevacizumab improves pfs_months overall (positive main effect after adjusting for stage_iv and ecog_ps).
treatment_regorafenib has a different effect on pfs_months in stage_iv vs non-stage_iv patients (interaction).
Cetuximab requires KRAS wild-type AND NRAS wild-type (RAS WT) for benefit; effect is null/negative in any RAS-mutant patient.
Cetuximab requires KRAS wild-type AND NRAS wild-type AND BRAF wild-type for benefit (full RAS/RAF WT subgroup).

analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib main β=0.9411; interaction β=0.0696, p=0.151

The full responder subgroup for cetuximab is: kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided RAS/RAF WT).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a treatment_×_feature interaction on pfs_months for treatment_cetuximab that identifies a subgroup with disproportionate benefit (screen across binary features).
There is a treatment_×_feature interaction on pfs_months for treatment_bevacizumab that identifies a subgroup with disproportionate benefit (screen across binary features).
There is a treatment_×_feature interaction on pfs_months for treatment_pembrolizumab that identifies a subgroup with disproportionate benefit (screen across binary features).
There is a treatment_×_feature interaction on pfs_months for treatment_encorafenib that identifies a subgroup with disproportionate benefit (screen across binary features).
There is a treatment_×_feature interaction on pfs_months for treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib that identifies a subgroup with disproportionate benefit (screen across binary features).
There is a treatment_×_feature interaction on pfs_months for treatment_regorafenib that identifies a subgroup with disproportionate benefit (screen across binary features).
Pembrolizumab benefit (in MSI-high patients) is modified by ecog_ps: patients with poorer performance status get less benefit.
Encorafenib benefit (in BRAF V600E patients) is modified by stage_iv: benefit is smaller/absent in stage IV.
Trastuzumab+tucatinib benefit (in HER2-amplified patients) is modified by ecog_ps.
In RAS/RAF WT left-sided patients, cetuximab benefit is further modified by stage_iv (smaller in stage IV).

analyses.result_summary: Within MSI-H (n=2513): pembro β=0.075, ecog β=-1.178, interaction β=-0.054, p=0.752

proposed_hypotheses.text: After joint modeling, the treatment_cetuximab × kras_mutation, × nras_mutation, × braf_v600e, and × right_sided_primary interactions remain jointly significant predictors of pfs_months.
After joint modeling, the treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high interaction remains significant after adjusting for other treatment×biomarker terms.
After joint modeling, the treatment_encorafenib × braf_v600e interaction remains significant.
After joint modeling, the treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib × her2_amplified interaction remains significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete cetuximab responder subgroup is: kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided RAS/RAF WT). Within this subgroup, mean pfs_months is higher in cetuximab-treated patients.
Removing any of these four predicates (KRAS WT, NRAS WT, BRAF WT, left-sided) substantially reduces cetuximab's PFS benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete pembrolizumab responder subgroup is msi_high=1; within MSI-H, pembrolizumab-treated patients have markedly higher pfs_months than untreated.
Within MSI-H, pembrolizumab benefit is modulated by stage_iv (smaller in stage IV) — refining the subgroup to msi_high=1 AND stage_iv=0 may show an even larger effect.
Pembrolizumab benefit is strongest in MSI-H AND ecog_ps≤1 (excluding very poor PS).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS, lower albumin_g_dl, higher ldh_u_l, higher crp_mg_l, higher nlr, higher cea_ng_ml, higher ecog_ps, and stage_iv=1 each independently shorten pfs_months.
Outside MSI-H, pembrolizumab is associated with shorter or no-better pfs_months (potential confounding/treatment in unselected patients yielding null/negative effect).
Outside BRAF V600E, encorafenib is associated with no benefit on pfs_months.
Outside HER2 amplification, trastuzumab+tucatinib has no benefit on pfs_months.
In KRAS-mutant or NRAS-mutant or BRAF-mutant patients, cetuximab has no PFS benefit (or negative effect).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for ecog_ps, stage_iv, age_years, sex_female, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, treatment_bevacizumab has a positive independent effect on pfs_months.
After the same adjustment, treatment_regorafenib has a non-positive (null or negative) independent effect on pfs_months.

In a joint OLS that includes all six treatments and their key biomarker matches, each targeted treatment's biomarker-matched interaction (cetuximab×KRAS WT proxy via -kras, pembrolizumab×msi_high, encorafenib×braf_v600e, trastuzumab+tucatinib×her2_amplified) is positive and the matching simple effects on the rest of the population are smaller.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Exhaustive search of small multi-feature subgroups confirms that left-sided RAS/RAF wild-type defines cetuximab's responder population.
Exhaustive search confirms msi_high defines pembrolizumab's responder population, with no further enhancement from any other binary feature.
Regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is concentrated in a subgroup that includes kras_mutation=0 (KRAS wild-type); within KRAS-mutant patients, regorafenib has no PFS benefit.
Regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is concentrated in left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0); within right-sided primaries, regorafenib has no benefit (or harmful effect).
Regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is concentrated in BRAF wild-type (braf_v600e=0); within BRAF V600E patients, regorafenib has no benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final cetuximab responder subgroup: kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0; treatment_cetuximab=1 increases pfs_months (positive direction).
Final pembrolizumab responder subgroup: msi_high=1; treatment_pembrolizumab=1 increases pfs_months (positive direction).
Final encorafenib responder subgroup: braf_v600e=1; treatment_encorafenib=1 increases pfs_months.
Final trastuzumab+tucatinib responder subgroup: her2_amplified=1; treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 increases pfs_months.

Final regorafenib responder subgroup: kras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND braf_v600e=0 (left-sided KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT). Within this subgroup, treatment_regorafenib=1 increases pfs_months substantially (signed positive).
Within RAS WT (KRAS=0 & NRAS=0) AND BRAF WT AND left-sided, treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months (the most refined responder definition).

Removing the KRAS-WT predicate, the BRAF-WT predicate, OR the left-sided predicate from the regorafenib responder subgroup definition substantially reduces (or abolishes) the regorafenib treatment effect.

analyses.result_summary: FINAL treatment_cetuximab effect in RAS/RAF WT, left-sided (n=16443): treated PFS=4.617 (n=5067) vs untreated=4.684 (n=11376); diff=-0.067, p=0.125
FINAL treatment_pembrolizumab effect in MSI-H (n=2513): treated PFS=4.299 (n=396) vs untreated=4.292 (n=2117); diff=0.007, p=0.957

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the responder subgroup defined in h24.1, treatment_cetuximab does NOT increase pfs_months (null or negative).
Outside MSI-H, treatment_pembrolizumab does NOT increase pfs_months.
Outside BRAF V600E, treatment_encorafenib does NOT increase pfs_months.
Outside HER2-amplified, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib does NOT increase pfs_months.
Outside the regorafenib responder subgroup defined in h24.5 (i.e., right-sided OR KRAS-mutant OR BRAF-V600E), treatment_regorafenib has no PFS benefit (or negative effect).

analyses.result_summary: OUTSIDE-subgroup treatment_cetuximab in non-responder superset (n=33557): treated PFS=4.117 (n=10011) vs untreated=4.149 (n=23546); diff=-0.032, p=0.207
OUTSIDE-subgroup treatment_pembrolizumab in MSI-stable (n=47487): treated PFS=4.317 (n=7264) vs untreated=4.312 (n=40223); diff=0.005, p=0.861
OUTSIDE-subgroup treatment_encorafenib in BRAF WT (n=47728): treated PFS=4.333 (n=4869) vs untreated=4.321 (n=42859); diff=0.013, p=0.718
OUTSIDE-subgroup treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib in HER2-non-amplified (n=48496): treated PFS=4.271 (n=3913) vs untreated=4.317 (n=44583); diff=-0.046, p=0.232
OUTSIDE regorafenib responder subgroup (right-sided OR KRAS-mut OR BRAF-V600E, n=32628): treated PFS=4.147 (n=6561) vs untreated=4.117 (n=26067); diff=0.030, p=0.294
